doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules on series 5 are as follows:  
Right lower lobe:  
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 4 mm on image 137 (no change).  
In both lungs, micronodules measuring up to 2–3 mm (no change). In both lungs, mild paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending artery, qualitatively mild.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Bilateral mammoplasty.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
doc_impression:
1. Solid nodule in the right lower lobe and micronodules in both lungs, stable compared with March 26, 2025; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no additional CT follow-up is recommended. Mild paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema in both lungs.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Bilateral mammoplasty.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

val_impression:
1. A few nodules in both lungs, stable compared with March 26, 2025; per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, no additional CT follow-up is recommended.

2. Mild paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening.

3. Coronary calcification is present in the left anterior descending artery, qualitatively mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Lung nodule, emphysema, former smoker for 25+ years, quit 14 years ago.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on March 26, 2025.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 7.84 mGy; total DLP 300.78 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
INDICATION: Shortness of breath for 1 year.

FINDINGS:
Lungs and pleura: The lungs are expanded. No emphysematous changes are identified. No focal areas of alveolar filling or consolidation are identified. No findings of interstitial lung disease, honeycombing, or pulmonary fibrosis. No pleural effusion. A single solid nodule in the left upper lobe in segment 4 measures 3 mm (series 4 image 190). On the right, solid polygonal nodules: along the interlobar pleura measuring less than 3 mm (series 4 image 155), along the pleura measuring 4 mm (series 4 image 232), likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mediastinal mass. A single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis (series 3 image 113). No enlarged lymph nodes in the lung hila, within limitations related to the lack of contrast. Several small (shotty) lymph nodes in both axillary regions, unchanged.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. No significant coronary artery calcification is identified. Mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: No aortic aneurysm. Pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. Single calcifications of the aortic arch and the infrarenal aorta.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. A metallic rod is present in the proximal left humerus. Bilateral breast implants are present.

Upper abdomen: Multiple bilobar liver lesions are identified: hypoattenuating, homogeneous, with density up to 12 HU, measuring from 4 mm to 4.7 cm in diameter, largest in segment 2. In one lesion in segment 3 measuring 3.2 cm in diameter, single mural calcifications are present. The adrenal glands are not enlarged, additional lesions are identified.
doc_impression:
1. Single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measuring up to 1.2 cm.

2. Single solid nodule in the left upper lobe. No follow-up is required per the updated Fleischner Society guideline 2017. A few small solid nodules, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

3. Mild aortic calcification.

4. Mitral valve calcification.

5. Multiple liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts. No follow-up is required per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017.

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

7. Postoperative changes in the proximal left humerus.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral scarring. Single area of paraseptal emphysema on the left (series 4 image 56). In the subpleural lower areas, more on the right, there is thickening of the interlobular interstitium. Osteophyte-induced adjacent fibrosis.
In the lung parenchyma, nodules are identified, in series 4 presented as follows:
In the right middle lobe:
- Solid nodule along the interlobar pleura measures 3 mm (image 155), likely perifissural lymph node, requires no follow-up.
- Triangular subpleural nodule measures 4 mm (image 232).
In the left upper lobe:
- Triangular solid nodule measures 3 mm (image 190), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
In the left lower lobe: 
- Triangular solid nodule measures 4 mm (image 194), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
- Triangular solid nodule measures 4 mm (image 217), likely intrapulmonary lymph node, requires no follow-up.
No pleural effusion. 

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mediastinal mass. A single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis (series 3 image 113).

Heart: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left heart. No significant pericardial effusion. Single calcification is  identified in the left anterior descending coronary artery, qualitatively assessed as mild. Mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: No aortic aneurysm. Pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. Single calcifications of the aortic arch and the infrarenal aorta.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. metallic hardware is present in the proximal left humerus. Bilateral breast implants are present with hypodense areas in the structure.

Upper abdomen: Multiple liver lesions are identified: hypoattenuating, homogeneous, with density up to 12 HU, measuring from 4 mm to 4.7 cm in diameter, largest in segment 2. In one lesion in segment 3 measuring 3.2 cm in diameter, single mural calcifications are present.
val_impression:
1. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy in the form of a single enlarged paraaortic lymph node measuring up to 1.2 cm.

2. Multiple triangular solid nodules in both lungs, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes. No follow-up is required per the updated Fleischner Society guideline 2017. 

3. Subpleural interstitial changes (thickening of the interlobular interstitium) may be cardiogenic or lymphatic associated.

4. Single calcification is identified in the left anterior descending coronary artery, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Mitral valve calcification. Mild aortic calcification.

6. Multiple liver lesions with benign features, likely cysts. No follow-up is required per Management of Incidental Liver Lesions on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2017.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Shortness of breath for 1 year.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 4.50 mGy; total DLP 171.40 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:

Right middle lobe:
- Centrilobular micronodules measuring up to 3 mm on images 102-118.

In the left upper lobe, there is a subpleural area of consolidation, series 5, images 110-139. In the left lower lobe, there is a subpleural area of consolidation, series 5, images 88-119. In the right middle lobe, bronchiectasis is present in the setting of the above-described areas of consolidation. In both lungs, there are solid micronodules measuring up to 3 mm. In both lungs, there is mild paraseptal emphysema. In both lungs, there are discoid atelectases. In the right lower lobe, there is a calcified granuloma measuring up to 3 mm. In the left pleural space, there is a small amount of effusion with a layer thickness up to 1.5 cm, volume about 116 mL. Bronchial walls are unevenly thickened, with bronchial contents in the lumens of some bronchi (predominantly at the level of the right middle and lower lobes).

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass lesion or lymphadenopathy is identified. At the level of the mid third of the esophagus along the right semicircumference, there is a wall diverticulum measuring up to 2.4 × 1.6 cm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary artery bypass grafting.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Diffuse uneven decreased density of vertebral bodies (maximally up to 72 HU). Sternotomy.

Upper abdomen: In the liver, there is a calcification measuring up to 6 mm. In the gallbladder lumen, there is wall-adherent dense contents.
doc_impression:
1.

Micronodules in both lungs. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, in high-risk patients follow-up chest CT in 12 months is recommended; in low-risk patients, no follow-up is required. Centrilobular micronodules in the right middle lobe may correspond to bronchiolitis. Areas of consolidation in the left lung require differentiation between sequelae of prior inflammatory changes versus the course of left-sided polysegmental pneumonia. Bronchial secretions and bronchial wall thickening in both lungs, which may correspond to bronchitis. Mild paraseptal emphysema in both lungs. Bronchiectasis and discoid atelectasis in both lungs. Calcified granuloma in the right lower lobe, consistent with prior infection.

2.

Small-volume left pleural effusion.

3.

Esophageal wall diverticulum.

4.

Moderate atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5.

Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe. Coronary artery bypass grafting.

6.

Findings consistent with osteoporosis. Sternotomy.

7.

Hepatic calcification; no further workup is required.

8.

Dense contents in the gallbladder lumen.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

val_protocol:
INDICATION: LUNG NODULE. ABNORMAL XR.

COMPARISON: Chest Xray 10/15/2025.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 14.45 mGy; total DLP 517.25 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura:
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases bilaterally.
There are persistent nodules of varying density in the lungs, and a focal area of consolidation in the right upper lobe:

In the right lung:
- In the upper lobe:
  - Focal consolidation with linear margins, adjacent to the apical pleura, measuring about 3.7 × 2.7 cm (no change), with traction and deformation of the adjacent small bronchi (the lumens of the latter are not visualized within the lesion).
  - Subpleural solid nodule measuring up to 4 mm, partially calcified, polygonal in shape (series 5, image 101), with no change.
  Subsolid nodule measuring up to 4 mm (series 5, image 70), with no change.
- In the middle lobe:
  - Perifissural solid nodule measuring up to 8 mm, with no change.

In the left lung:
- In the upper lobe:
  - Subpleural (series 5, image 47) ground-glass nodule of moderate intensity, measuring about 1.7 × 1.4 cm, with no change.
  - Subsolid nodule adjacent to the interlobar pleura (the latter is retracted), measuring about 1.4 × 1.1 cm; margins are linear, with no change.
- In the lower lobe:
  - Subpleural solid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (series 5, image 120), with no change.

No pleural effusion.
The trachea and central bronchi (except as described above) are patent; walls are thickened.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass is identified. Station 7 lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis, with no change.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.0 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone tissue density in vertebrae at the T11-L2 level down to +113 HU (CT signs of osteopenia). Osteoscleroic lesions in the posterior segment of the left 8th rib measuring up to 9 mm, with no change.

Upper abdomen:
In the renal parenchyma, hypodense lesions with sharp smooth margins and fluid density measure up to 5.9 × 5.7 cm on the right and 3.8 × 3.1 cm on the left (no change).
Increase in size of a lymph node along the hepatoduodenal ligament to 1.2 cm in short axis, previously up to 8 mm.
Wall calcification of the abdominal aorta and its branches at the imaged level.
Increase in the splenic index to 650, with mild interval increase.
Portal vein dilation to 1.7 cm (no change), and splenic vein dilation to 1.0 cm (previously up to 9 mm).
doc_impression:
1. CT signs of focal apical right lung consolidation, with no change since 2024; correlation with clinical history is required to differentiate primarily between scarring, post-therapeutic changes (as a consequence of antitumor therapy) versus a lung mass with stable size. Fleischner Society recommendations from 2017 may not be applicable considering the clinical history. CT signs of pulmonary nodules of varying density, with no change. CT signs of bronchial wall thickening (may be a sign of bronchitis).

2. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Aortic valve calcification.

5. Thoracic aortic atherosclerosis.

6. Single enlarged intrathoracic lymph node (station 7), with no change.

7. Enlarged lymph node along the hepatoduodenal ligament (previously smaller), increased spleen size, dilation of the portal (stable) and splenic (with mild increase) veins; to clarify the nature of the changes, an abdominal organ study and correlation with follow-up studies are recommended. Hypodense renal lesions of fluid density are stable (likely cysts): per Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2018, CT follow-up is not required.

8. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization) at T11-L2. Osteoscleroic lesions in the left 8th rib, with no change (likely degenerative).

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: In the apex of the right lung, a spiculated solid mass measures 3.4 × 2.3 × 1.7 cm, with strands to the pleura, with a single 1 mm calcification, deformation and interruption of the small bronchi. On December 11, 2024, the mass measured 3.1 × 2.4 × 1.8 cm, had a larger number of spicules and a ground-glass area on the periphery, and was without calcification.
In the left upper lobe, a subpleural area of ground-glass is preserved (series 5, image 47), measuring about 1.7 × 1.4 cm, with no change. 
An area of consolidation and ground glass in the left upper lobe adjacent to the interlobar pleura (series 5, images 66-83), with small bronchiectasis, measures 2.0 × 1.1 cm (previously 1.5 × 1.0 cm). The consolidation part has increased in comparison with chest CT on December 11, 2024. 
In the lower parts, there is thickening of the interlobular interstitium, reticular changes, and mosaic pneumatization. On the right, there are supradiaphragmatic centrilobular ground-glass nodules (tree-in-bud) (series 5, images 198-217), which are not previously identified. 
In the lung parenchyma, multiple nodules are identified, in series 5 presented as follows:
 In the right upper lobe:
- Calcified nodule measures 3 mm (series 5, image 30), with no change.
- Subpleural ground-glass nodule measures 3 mm (series 5, image 71), with no change.
In the right middle lobe:
- Perifissural solid nodule measures up to 8 mm (series 5, image 157), with no change.
In the right lower lobe:
 - A few subpleural solid nodules measure up to 3 mm (series 5, images 157-161), with no change.
In the left upper lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (series 5, image 163), with no change.
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 6 mm (series 5, image 120), with no change.
Multiple calcified and solid micronodules up to 2 mm in both lungs with no change. 
No pleural effusion. Wall thickening of the subsegmental and segmental bronchi with secretion. The wall of the trachea and large bronchi is severely thickened.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass is identified. Subcarinal lymph node (station 7 lymph node) measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis, with no change. Other lymph nodes are multiple, up to 9 mm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.1 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone tissue density in vertebrae at the T11-L2 level down to +113 HU, numerous rounded areas of low density in the vertebral bodies, previously identified. Osteoscleroic lesions on the right at the 7th rib, on the left at the 8th rib, with no change.

Upper abdomen: In the renal parenchyma, hypodense lesions with smooth margins and fluid density measure up to 5.9 × 5.7 cm on the right and 3.8 × 3.1 cm on the left (no change). Increase in size of a lymph node along the hepatoduodenal ligament to 1.2 cm in short axis, previously up to 8 mm. Increased spleen size, compared to December 11, 2024. Wall calcification of the abdominal aorta and its branches. Portal vein dilation to 1.7 cm (no change), and splenic vein dilation to 1.0 cm (previously up to 9 mm).
val_impression:
1. In the apex of the right lung, neoplasm without signs of growth, with slight positive dynamics compared to CT on December 11, 2024. Subcarinal lymph node enlargement, with no change.

2. Interval worsening in the form of an increase in the size of the consolidation area in the left upper lobe and the appearance of bronchiolitis in the supradiaphragmatic regions on the right. These changes may represent an inflammatory process. 
Multiple nodules with no change. Fleischner Society recommendations may not be applicable considering the clinical history. Follow-up per oncology follow-up protocol.

3. Bronchial secretion and wall thickening may represent bronchitis. Wall thickening of the trachea and large bronchi may correspond to the large airway disease.

4. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

5. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.1 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

6. Enlarged lymph node along the hepatoduodenal ligament (previously smaller), increased spleen size, dilation of the portal (stable) and splenic (with mild increase) veins. Follow-up per oncology follow-up protocol.
Hypodense renal lesions of fluid density are stable (likely cysts). Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee, 2018 may not be applicable considering the clinical history.


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: History of lung cancer. History of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma of the abdomen.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on June 26, 2024, December 11, 2024. 

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 11.38 mGy; total DLP 471.82 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases on the left. Single calcifications in the lungs up to 3 mm. Pulmonary parenchymal nodules persist and in series 5 are as follows:

In the right lung:
— In the upper lobe:
* Perifissural nodule measuring up to 4 mm, polygonal shape (image 105), unchanged.
* Nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 52), unchanged.

In the left lung:
— In the upper lobe:
* Peribronchial nodules with a tree-in-bud pattern, measuring up to 4 mm (images 74-82), previously of lower density and smaller size (up to 4 mm).
Peribronchovascular nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 123), unchanged.
— In the lower lobe:
* Subpleural subsolid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 97), previously up to 8 mm.

No pleural effusion.
Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.4 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the L1 level to +130 HU (CT signs of osteopenia).

Upper abdomen: Thickening of the body of the left adrenal gland up to 1.1 cm.

Thyroid gland: Heterogeneous due to nodules in both lobes with indistinct margins measuring up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm.
doc_impression:
1. CT appearance of single pulmonary nodules: compared with the prior study, there is decreased size of the subsolid lesion in the left lower lobe, increased density of its structure, and increased nodules characteristic of bronchiolitis in the left upper lobe; the above changes may correspond to postinflammatory changes and residual inflammatory changes, respectively. The remaining single solid pulmonary nodules are unchanged. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT follow-up in 6 months is recommended.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.4 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis at the L1 level.

6. Thickening of the left adrenal gland (may correspond to hyperplasia): per Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee 2017, CT follow-up is not required.

7. Nodular changes of the thyroid gland: per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases, clinically insignificant. Calcified nodules in both lungs up to 3 mm, unchanged, require no follow-up. Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
In the right upper lobe:
- Perifissural node measures up to 4 mm (image 105), unchanged, requires no follow-up.
- Solid nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 52), unchanged.
In the left upper lobe:
- Centrilobular nodules with a tree-in-bud pattern measure up to 4 mm (images 74-82), previously of lower density (ground glass) and smaller size (up to 3 mm).
- Elongated nodule measures up to 6 mm (image 123), unchanged, probably bronchocele.
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural solid nodule measures up to 4 mm (image 97), previously up to 5 mm.
No pleural effusion. Trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Moderate coronary calcification. 

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated, with moderate wall calcification. Main pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.4 cm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the L1 level to +130 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Degenerative changes in the right shoulder joint with large subchondral cysts of the scapula and humeral head.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable. 

Thyroid: Thyroid is heterogeneous due to nodules in both lobes measuring up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm.
val_impression:
1. Centrilobular nodules in the left upper lobe: compared with the prior study, there is decreased size and increased density of them, correspond to post-inflammatory changes of the bronchiolitis. Elongated nodule in the left upper lobe is unchanged, probably bronchocele. Solid and calcified nodules in both lungs are unchanged. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: No need for follow-up.

2. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.4 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension. Consider cardiac echo.

3. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. 

4. Aortic valve calcification. 

5. Multiple thyroid nodules measure up to 1.7 × 1.4 cm: per Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee 2014, thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_protocol:
INDICATION: Lung nodules.

COMPARISON: Chest CT on October 2, 2025. 

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 9.67 mGy; total DLP 299.64 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 1.
